summary: Matched ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag in ftCommon damage code while renaming SDI-related fighter/common-data fields and propagating those names through fighter damage, guard, collision, and helper code. The diff removes a SOLUTION macro branch and M2C_FIELD alternatives in favor of typed fields, GET_FIGHTER, vector/square macros, and semantic SDI field names. Bot report records 1 new match for main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage::ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag, 86.07% to 100.00%, +26 bytes.

key_files.role: Primary matched function; rewrote ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag and updated related SDI field references.
Renamed ftCommonData fields at +4B0/+4B4/+4B8 and Fighter bitfield at fp+221A:2 to SDI-related names.
Propagated allow_sdi and sdi_* field names into guard setoff/ground SDI logic.
Propagated allow_sdi bitfield name through fighter reset, hitlag, and post-hitlag control paths.
Propagated allow_sdi name into collision handling branches.
Propagated sdi_min_stick_mag name into c-stick SDI threshold helper.

what_changed: ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag was reduced from a nested generated-looking implementation with a SOLUTION macro and conditional M2C_FIELD access to a direct condition using GET_FIGHTER, allow_sdi, VEC2_SQ_LEN, SQ, and typed timer fields.
Raw or placeholder common-data fields p_ftCommonData->x4B0, x4B4, and x4B8 were renamed to sdi_min_stick_mag, sdi_stick_window, and sdi_pos_scale in types.h and at changed call sites.
Fighter bitfield x221A_b2 was renamed to allow_sdi in types.h and propagated through damage, guard, fighter, and collision code.
The old equality threshold check in OnEveryHitlag was replaced by a squared-length greater-than-or-equal check against SQ(sdi_min_stick_mag).
The prior 0xFE assignments became decimal 254 assignments for x670_timer_lstick_tilt_x and x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y.
Bot report states ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag reached 100.00% for its unit, with 1 new match and +180 matched-code bytes overall.

smart_moves.text: Removed the temporary SOLUTION macro and alternate M2C_FIELD branches once typed fields were available, making the matched source easier to review.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed #define SOLUTION/#undef SOLUTION and #if SOLUTION blocks

smart_moves.text: Renamed related fields at the struct definition and updated multiple users in the same PR, avoiding a mismatch between semantic names in one function and anonymous offsets elsewhere.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_Damage.c, ftCo_Guard.c, fighter.c, ft_081B.c, ft_0DF1.c hunks

smart_moves.text: Used existing expression/helper macros where they expressed the operation, including GET_FIGHTER, VEC2_SQ_LEN, and SQ.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched function exposes the meaning of anonymous fields, propagate supported names through the owning type and nearby users rather than leaving mixed semantic and x-offset names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: allow_sdi and sdi_* propagation hunks

decomp_lessons.lesson: Temporary match scaffolding such as SOLUTION switches and M2C_FIELD alternatives should be removed once typed field access gives a clean match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed SOLUTION macro and M2C_FIELD branches

decomp_lessons.lesson: For stick-magnitude checks, project macros like SQ and VEC2_SQ_LEN can express intent and produce reviewable matching source when supported by the diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag and ftCo_800DF608 SQ uses

naming_conventions.name: allow_sdi
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Repeated use as a gate for SDI-related damage, guard, collision, and hitlag behavior in the diff.

naming_conventions.name: sdi_min_stick_mag
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Used as the squared stick/c-stick threshold in SDI-related checks.

naming_conventions.name: sdi_stick_window
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Compared against x670/x671 stick tilt timers before applying SDI movement.

naming_conventions.name: sdi_pos_scale
naming_conventions.meaning_supported_by: Scales stick input before adding to fighter position in SDI-related movement.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Collapse nested generated-looking branches into a single compound if condition for the matched hitlag function.
Replace manual x*x+y*y code with VEC2_SQ_LEN and SQ macros where the operation is a vector magnitude threshold.
Use typed Fighter fields and bitfields instead of M2C_FIELD offset access for timers and flags.
Use GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in the matched function instead of direct gobj->user_data access.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Remove temporary match scaffolding after typed fields are recovered
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2198 matched ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag by replacing a SOLUTION macro, conditional M2C_FIELD offset access, and anonymous fields with typed Fighter fields, semantic SDI names, and existing macros.

accepted_candidate_records.title: SDI-related fighter/common-data field names
accepted_candidate_records.text: The diff supports allow_sdi for Fighter fp+221A:2 and sdi_min_stick_mag/sdi_stick_window/sdi_pos_scale for ftCommonData +4B0/+4B4/+4B8 within fighter SDI-related checks.

source_update_candidates.title: Fighter SDI naming is centralized in types.h and used across ftCommon damage/guard paths
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2198 changed src/melee/ft/types.h so Fighter fp+221A:2 is allow_sdi and ftCommonData +4B0/+4B4/+4B8 are sdi_min_stick_mag, sdi_stick_window, and sdi_pos_scale, then propagated those names through ftCo_Damage.c, ftCo_Guard.c, fighter.c, ft_081B.c, and ft_0DF1.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path fact for future fighter SDI decomp work; should be curated as source-owned path knowledge rather than inferred globally.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that all gobj->user_data accesses should become GET_FIGHTER; this PR only shows that change inside ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag.
Do not infer a broad rule preferring decimal 254 over 0xFE; the diff only shows this constant form in this function.
Do not treat the dismissed 'Thanks!' review as a detailed approval rationale.
